serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of beetroot salad is approximately 150 g, equivalent to a small side-dish bowl.

nutrient_source.title: Beetroot Salad — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Foodo record for Beetroot Salad (cr_id G00010349). All per-100g values preserved from the canonical HPB record. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central olive oil composition (approx. 14% of total fat is saturated) given HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown for this entry.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 64 assigned based on beetroot (boiled) from the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al., 2008; Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283). The salad is predominantly beetroot with olive oil dressing; olive oil has negligible carbohydrates and does not materially alter GI. GL per serving calculated as: GI × available carbohydrate per serving (g) / 100 = 64 × (5.9 × 1.5) / 100 = 64 × 8.85 / 100 ≈ 5.7.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 810 mg per 100 g — exceeds the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold for hypertension-sensitive foods
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this preparation; reducing added salt to below 200 mg sodium per 100 g would allow beetroot's dietary nitrates to exert their vasodilatory benefit without the counterproductive sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate GI (~64) offset by low glycaemic load (GL ≈ 5.7 per 150 g serving) and fat-slowed gastric emptying from olive oil
type2_diabetes.guidance: A standard 150 g serving carries a low glycaemic load of approximately 5.7, making this dish acceptable for most people with type 2 diabetes when portion size is controlled; however, the high caloric density (430 kcal/100 g) from olive oil warrants mindful portioning to avoid excess energy intake.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium 810 mg/100 g is a primary cardiovascular risk factor; high total fat (44.5 g/100 g) though predominantly monounsaturated from olive oil
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The very high sodium content (810 mg/100 g) poses a significant cardiovascular risk and outweighs the cardioprotective benefit of olive oil's monounsaturated fatty acids; this dish should be reformulated with minimal added salt before being recommended for individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Sodium 810 mg/100 g exceeds the 400 mg/100 g RA threshold; saturated fat ~6.2 g/100 g may promote pro-inflammatory cytokine activity
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this high-sodium preparation; reducing salt and retaining beetroot (betalains) and olive oil (oleocanthal) would convert this dish into an anti-inflammatory option.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Beetroot Salad is a Middle Eastern-style vegetable dish made from cooked (boiled or roasted) beetroot dressed generously with olive oil, garlic, lemon juice, and salt. The dish is served as a cold or room-temperature side salad, common at lunch and dinner across Middle Eastern and Mediterranean cuisines, and is also found on Singaporean menus as part of a mezze or salad spread. The texture is tender and slightly earthy from the beetroot, with a rich, unctuous mouthfeel from the olive oil. The flavour profile is mildly sweet from the beetroot's natural sugars (4.7 g/100 g), balanced by the sharpness of garlic and lemon. Nutritionally, this preparation is calorie-dense at 430 kcal per 100 g due to the high olive oil content (total fat 44.5 g/100 g, predominantly monounsaturated). Protein is low at 1.3 g/100 g and carbohydrates are modest at 5.9 g/100 g. The primary clinical concern is the very high sodium content of 810 mg/100 g, which disqualifies this preparation for individuals managing hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis without significant salt reduction. Beetroot is a natural source of dietary nitrates, which support endothelial function and blood pressure regulation, but this benefit is negated by the excessive added salt in the current formulation. The glycaemic index of beetroot is approximately 64 (medium), but the glycaemic load per 150 g serving is low at approximately 5.7, making it suitable for type 2 diabetes management when portion-controlled. Dietary fibre is reported as 0.0 g/100 g in the HPB source, which is atypical for beetroot and may reflect the specific preparation or analytical method used.
common_user_queries: Is beetroot salad good for diabetics?
Is beetroot salad high in sodium?
Can I eat beetroot salad if I have high blood pressure?
How many calories in beetroot salad?
What is the GI of beetroot salad?
Is beetroot salad good for heart health?
What are the ingredients in Middle Eastern beetroot salad?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the canonical HPB Foodo record (cr_id G00010349, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat and fatty acid breakdown estimated from USDA FoodData Central olive oil data and documented accordingly. GI sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database via Atkinson et al. (2008).
notes: dietary_fibre_g is reported as 0.0 g/100 g in the HPB source; this is confirmed zero per the HPB canonical record, though it is atypical for cooked beetroot (which normally contains ~2–3 g/100 g fibre) and may reflect the specific preparation or the analytical method applied to this dish formulation. Saturated fat (6.24 g/100 g), monounsaturated fat (32.5 g/100 g), and polyunsaturated fat (4.7 g/100 g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central olive oil fatty acid profile (USDA SR Legacy, NDB 04053) applied proportionally to the total fat of 44.54 g/100 g; HPB does not report fatty acid fractions for this entry. trans_fat_g set to 0.0 as olive oil contains negligible trans fats — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed micronutrient and moisture data for this specific HPB beetroot salad preparation are not reported in the HPB Foodo database as of 2026. Cholesterol at 26 mg/100 g is preserved from the HPB source; this is unusual for a plant-based olive oil dish and may reflect a specific preparation variant or analytical artefact — flagged for review. The alias '6. Beetroot Salad' is preserved from the input record's primary alias.
